scenario: Orion Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS with 9500 policies per day. Before confirming a migration of 75 active instances of orderProcess, the operator wants to double-check how the source flow node Check payment is visually mapped to its counterpart on version 2 of orderProcess in the migration view.
question: How can the operator visualize how an individual source flow node is mapped to its target in the migration view?

options.text: Open the operations panel after starting the migration, where the operator can confirm or reject each per-flow-node mapping individually for the migration plan
Switch to Optimize and run the Migration Mapping report, which lists every source-to-target mapping for the selected process versions in the cluster setup
Click a flow node on the diagram, or click a source flow node row in the bottom panel, to see how that flow node is currently mapped to the target in the view
Hover over the Migrate button in the toolbar; this surfaces a tooltip with the full list of source-to-target mappings for the operation across the migration plan

a.text: Incorrect. Per-flow-node confirmation does not happen via the operations panel after starting the migration; the mappings are inspected in the migration view itself before starting.

b.text: Incorrect. Optimize is analytics-only; the migration view itself surfaces how a specific source flow node maps to its target via the diagram and the bottom panel before the migration starts.

c.text: Correct. The migration guide says the operator can click a flow node on the diagram or click a source flow node row in the bottom panel to see how that flow node is mapped to the target.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no documented Migrate-button tooltip listing all mappings; mappings are inspected in the diagram and the bottom flow node mapping panel of the migration view itself.

explanation: Per the migration guide, the operator can preview how an individual source flow node is mapped to its target by clicking the flow node on the diagram or by clicking the source flow node row in the bottom mapping panel.
